wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify project overview section focused on introducing the real-time chat application. Explain its purpose as a modern full-stack messaging platform, core architecture combining Spring Boot backend with React frontend, and relationship to real-time communication technologies. Document the implementation approach using WebSocket/STOMP for instant messaging, JWT authentication for security, and event-driven architecture with Kafka. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining chat concepts and technical details for experienced developers covering the complete tech stack. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like chat rooms, message delivery receipts, typing indicators, and presence tracking. Provide practical examples demonstrating the application's capabilities and common use cases. Document the overall system design, key features, and technology choices that make Chatify a robust real-time communication platform.

wiki_catalogs.name: Introduction and Purpose
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify introduction and purpose section focused on explaining what Chatify is and why it was built. Describe Chatify as a modern real-time chat application designed to provide seamless communication experiences through instant messaging, group conversations, and file sharing capabilities. Explain the application's core mission to deliver reliable, secure, and scalable chat solutions for individuals and teams. Document the target audience including developers, teams, and organizations seeking a robust chat platform. Include the problem Chatify solves in the real-time communication space, highlighting challenges with existing chat solutions. Provide context about the evolution of chat applications and Chatify's position in the modern web ecosystem. Use clear, accessible language for beginners while establishing the technical foundation for understanding the application's architecture and capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify backend application structure focused on the layered architecture and package organization. Explain the Spring Boot application initialization, main entry point configuration, and component scanning setup. Document the layered architecture pattern with clear separation between presentation (controllers), business logic (services), and data access (repositories) layers. Detail the configuration classes including SecurityConfig for authentication and authorization, WebMvcConfig for CORS and MVC settings, and WebSocketConfig for real-time communication setup. Include the application startup sequence, bean initialization order, and dependency injection patterns. Provide practical examples of how components interact within the layered architecture and explain the rationale behind the architectural decisions. Document the package structure organization and naming conventions used throughout the codebase.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure and Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify frontend application structure focused on React 19 application setup and routing configuration. Explain the component hierarchy starting from App.jsx which serves as the root component with BrowserRouter wrapping the entire application. Document the routing configuration using React Router with protected routes through PrivateRoute component, authentication routes (/login, /register, /auth/callback), and chat routes with dynamic parameters (:chatId). Detail the Vite build system configuration including plugins, aliases, and development server settings. Explain the main.jsx entry point and how it renders the React application. Include the toast notification system configuration with react-hot-toast for user feedback. Document the application initialization process, provider setup order (AuthProvider -> WebSocketProvider), and global styling approach. Provide practical examples of route protection, dynamic routing patterns, and component composition. Address development vs production build differences and environment variable handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Authentication API endpoints focused on user registration, login, OAuth2 token exchange, refresh token management, and logout functionality. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/auth/register, /api/auth/login, /api/auth/oauth2/token, /api/auth/refresh, /api/auth/logout), request/response schemas including UserRegistrationDTO, UserLoginDTO, and AuthResponseDTO structures. Explain JWT token handling, OAuth2 Google integration with HttpOnly cookie pattern, token validation, and refresh token mechanisms. Include authentication flow diagrams, security considerations for token storage, rate limiting strategies, and error handling patterns. Provide curl examples for each endpoint, client implementation guidelines for React frontend integration, and debugging approaches for authentication issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: STOMP Configuration and Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the STOMP (Simple Text Oriented Messaging Protocol) configuration and WebSocket endpoint setup. Explain the WebSocketConfig class implementation including endpoint registration with SockJS fallback support, CORS configuration for multiple origins, and message broker setup with topic and user destinations. Document the channel interceptor configuration for JWT authentication validation during WebSocket CONNECT frames, including token extraction, validation, and user authentication. Detail the heartbeat scheduler configuration for maintaining connection health and the thread pool scheduling for WebSocket operations. Include concrete examples showing endpoint URLs, allowed origins configuration, and authentication header handling. Explain the relationship between STOMP endpoints, message routing prefixes, and destination patterns. Address configuration options for production environments, security considerations, and performance tuning parameters.

wiki_catalogs.name: Entity Relationships and Modeling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Chatify entity relationship model focused on how core entities interact and maintain referential integrity. Detail the relationships between Message, ChatRoom, User, and UserChatState entities including foreign key constraints, cascade operations, and bidirectional associations. Explain the many-to-one relationships from Message to User (sender) and ChatRoom, the many-to-many relationship between Message and User through the message_read_by join table, and the one-to-many relationships from ChatRoom to Messages. Document the UserChatState entity that tracks user presence and chat room membership. Include entity relationship diagrams showing cardinalities and constraints. Address lazy loading strategies, fetch types, and performance implications of different relationship configurations. Explain how these relationships support real-time messaging features like read receipts, typing indicators, and presence tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the message state management system. Thoroughly explain the message state machine with SENT, DELIVERED, and SEEN states, including state transition logic and timing. Document the MessageStatus enum implementation and state validation rules. Detail the MessageType enum for different message categories (TEXT, FILE, SYSTEM). Explain delivery acknowledgment mechanisms through MessageDeliveryUpdateDTO and read receipt tracking via MessageSeenUpdateDTO and ReadReceiptDTO. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing state transitions from message creation to final read confirmation. Document the business logic for state updates, including edge cases like offline users and message expiration. Address state consistency across distributed systems and eventual consistency patterns. Provide implementation guidelines for extending state management with new message types and status transitions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the getting started section focused on helping developers quickly set up and run the Chatify application. Explain prerequisites including Java 17, Node.js 18, PostgreSQL 14, and Maven 3.6 requirements. Document the complete setup process including database configuration with SQL commands for creating the chatify database and user, backend setup with Maven commands and property file configuration, and frontend setup with npm installation and development server startup. Include environment variable configuration for both backend (application.properties) and frontend (.env) environments. Provide step-by-step instructions for running the application locally, accessing the web interface, and verifying successful installation. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting steps for database connectivity, port conflicts, and dependency problems. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features and Capabilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify key features and capabilities section focused on documenting all major functionalities of the real-time chat application. Detail the core messaging features including instant message delivery, real-time chat rooms, and multi-user conversations. Document advanced features such as message delivery receipts (DELIVERED/SEEN states), typing indicators, online presence tracking, and read acknowledgments. Explain file sharing capabilities including upload/download functionality, file size limits, and AWS S3 integration. Cover user management features including registration, authentication, OAuth2 Google integration, and user status management. Document group chat functionality with member management, permissions, and administrative controls. Include real-time features like WebSocket connectivity, automatic reconnection, and offline message handling. Provide practical examples demonstrating each feature's usage and benefits. Address both user-facing capabilities and underlying technical implementations that enable these features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication and security subsystem focused on JWT token management, OAuth2 Google integration, and custom user details service implementation. Thoroughly explain the JWT utility functions including token generation, validation, and refresh mechanisms. Document the JWT authentication filter implementation for extracting and validating tokens from HTTP requests. Detail the OAuth2 success handler for Google authentication flow and user registration process. Explain the custom user details service implementation for loading user information from the database. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing token-based authentication flow, user registration process, and login mechanisms. Document password encoding configuration, security configurations, and authentication error handling. Address common security issues and their solutions including token expiration, CSRF protection, and session management. Provide guidelines for extending the authentication system with additional providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Chatify component architecture focused on the modular React component organization and composition patterns. Document the chat component hierarchy including ChatWindow.jsx, MessageList.jsx, MessageItem.jsx, and supporting components like FileUpload.jsx and TypingIndicator.jsx. Explain the common components architecture with Avatar.jsx, LoadingSpinner.jsx, and OnlineStatus.jsx used across different parts of the application. Detail the sidebar component system including ChatList.jsx, ChatListItem.jsx, and NewChatModal.jsx for chat room management. Document the group management components CreateGroupModal.jsx and GroupInfo.jsx for group chat functionality. Explain the layout components and how they integrate with the overall application structure. Include component props interfaces, event handling patterns, and state management within components. Provide examples of component composition, conditional rendering, and responsive design implementation. Address component lifecycle management, performance optimization through memoization, and accessibility considerations. Document the virtualized rendering implementation in VirtualizedMessageList.jsx for large message lists.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the User Management API endpoints focused on user search, status updates, presence information, and online user retrieval. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/users/search, /api/users/status, /api/users/online), request/response schemas including UserDTO, OnlineStatusDTO, and UpdateStatusDTO structures. Explain user search functionality with query parameters, status update mechanisms, online/offline presence tracking, and user profile management. Include pagination support, filtering options, and sorting capabilities. Provide examples for user discovery, status broadcasting, and presence integration patterns. Address performance considerations for large user bases, caching strategies for user data, and real-time presence synchronization with WebSocket integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Handling and Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for WebSocket message handling and routing mechanisms. Explain the ChatWebSocketController implementation including inbound message processing, outbound message broadcasting, and subscription management for chat rooms and direct messages. Document the WebSocketEventListener for handling connection lifecycle events, user joins/leaves, and system notifications. Detail message routing patterns including topic-based broadcasting for chat rooms, user-specific messaging for direct conversations, and group message distribution. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing message format specifications, destination routing logic, and message acknowledgment patterns. Explain the integration with Kafka message processing pipeline for persistent message storage and real-time delivery. Document error handling strategies, message validation rules, and connection state management. Address performance considerations for high-volume messaging scenarios and message ordering guarantees.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Entity Definitions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the core database entities in the Chatify application. Document the Message entity with its content fields, timestamps, status tracking, and file attachment capabilities. Detail the ChatRoom entity including room metadata, member management, and group chat functionality. Explain the User entity covering authentication fields, profile information, status tracking, and OAuth2 integration. Document the UserChatState entity that manages user presence, online/offline status, and chat room membership states. For each entity, specify field definitions, data types, constraints, default values, and validation rules. Include JPA annotations and their purposes such as @CreationTimestamp, @Enumerated, and @ManyToMany. Provide sample data examples showing typical entity instances. Document indexing strategies, performance considerations, and query optimization patterns for each entity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Kafka Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Kafka integration in the message delivery system. Explain the event-driven architecture using ChatMessageEvent DTO for message serialization and deserialization. Document KafkaProducerService implementation including partition key strategy using chatRoomId for message ordering within rooms. Detail KafkaConsumerService configuration for message processing, including consumer groups, offset management, and error handling. Explain KafkaTopicConfig setup for chat-messages topic with appropriate partitions and replication factors. Document the asynchronous message publishing pattern using CompletableFuture for non-blocking operations. Include configuration examples for producer and consumer properties, retry mechanisms, and dead letter queues. Address message ordering guarantees, partition distribution, and scaling considerations. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common Kafka issues like consumer lag, topic configuration, and network connectivity problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Chatify backend system focused on the Spring Boot application structure and design patterns. Describe the high-level design using layered architecture with clear separation of concerns between presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Document component interactions including REST controllers, service layer implementations, JPA repositories, and configuration classes. Explain technical decisions around WebSocket/STOMP integration, JWT authentication, Kafka event processing, and database design. Include infrastructure requirements for PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, and AWS S3 integration. Provide system context diagrams showing how the backend handles real-time messaging, user authentication, file uploads, and presence tracking. Address cross-cutting concerns like security configuration, CORS handling, WebSocket authentication, and error handling strategies. Document the technology stack including Spring ecosystem dependencies, database schema design, and external service integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify technology stack overview section focused on documenting all technologies, frameworks, and tools used in the application. Detail the backend technology stack including Spring Boot 3.5.5, Java 17, Spring Security for authentication, Spring Data JPA for database operations, Spring WebSocket for real-time communication, Spring Kafka for event-driven architecture, PostgreSQL for data persistence, Redis for caching and presence tracking, and AWS SDK for cloud storage integration. Document the frontend technology stack including React 19, Vite for build tooling, Tailwind CSS for styling, Axios for HTTP requests, @stomp/stompjs for WebSocket communication, and sockjs-client for WebSocket fallback. Explain infrastructure technologies including Docker for containerization, Docker Compose for orchestration, and cloud services integration. Document development tools including Maven for dependency management, ESLint for code quality, and Postman for API testing. Include version compatibility information, rationale for technology choices, and how each technology contributes to the application's architecture and performance goals.

wiki_catalogs.name: WebSocket Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the WebSocket implementation focused on STOMP protocol configuration, real-time message handling, and connection management. Explain the WebSocket configuration including STOMP endpoint setup, security configuration with JWT authentication, and message broker configuration. Document the ChatWebSocketController implementation for handling real-time messaging, typing indicators, and presence updates. Detail the WebSocket event listener for managing user connections, disconnections, and room subscriptions. Include concrete examples of WebSocket message formats, subscription patterns, and real-time event broadcasting. Explain the integration between WebSocket communication and the Kafka message processing system for persistent message delivery. Document connection lifecycle management, error handling, and reconnection strategies. Address performance considerations for handling multiple concurrent WebSocket connections and scaling WebSocket-based real-time features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Context Providers and State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify context providers and state management system focused on React Context API implementation and custom hooks. Document the AuthContext.jsx provider responsible for authentication state management including user login/logout, JWT token handling, and OAuth2 callback processing. Explain the WebSocketContext.jsx provider for real-time communication state including connection management, message queuing, and automatic reconnection logic. Detail the custom hooks useAuth.js and useWebSocket.js that wrap the context providers for easier component integration. Include the state update patterns, context value structures, and how components consume context data. Explain the authentication flow from login to protected route access, token refresh mechanisms, and logout procedures. Document the WebSocket connection lifecycle including connection establishment, message broadcasting, error handling, and reconnection strategies. Provide practical examples of context consumption in components, state synchronization patterns, and error boundary implementations. Address performance considerations for context updates, memory management, and component re-render optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Chat Room API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Chat Room API endpoints focused on chat room creation, member management, and chat room operations. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/chatrooms, /api/chatrooms/{id}, /api/chatrooms/{id}/members), request/response schemas including ChatRoomDTO, CreateChatRequest, and member management structures. Explain private chat creation, group chat setup, member addition/removal, chat room metadata management, and permission controls. Include chat room lifecycle management, member role hierarchies, and chat room visibility settings. Provide examples for creating different chat types, managing group memberships, and handling chat room updates. Address concurrency considerations for member operations, chat room state consistency, and real-time synchronization with WebSocket updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Features Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for real-time feature implementations including typing indicators, read receipts, online presence tracking, and message delivery acknowledgments. Explain the TypingIndicatorDTO structure for real-time typing notifications, OnlineStatusDTO for user presence information, and UnreadCountDTO for message count tracking. Document the PresenceService implementation for managing user online/offline status, activity tracking, and presence broadcasting. Detail the integration patterns for real-time updates including WebSocket subscriptions, message broadcasting, and state synchronization across clients. Include concrete examples showing real-time feature activation, data transmission formats, and client-side update mechanisms. Explain the relationship between real-time features and backend services including Redis caching for presence data and message state management. Address performance optimization techniques for real-time updates, memory management for active connections, and scalability considerations for large user bases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Enum Types and Data Constraints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the enum types that define constrained data values across the Chatify application. Document MessageStatus enum with values like SENT, DELIVERED, SEEN and their role in the message delivery lifecycle. Explain MessageType enum covering TEXT, IMAGE, FILE, VIDEO content types and their impact on message processing. Detail UserStatus enum for online presence tracking including ONLINE, OFFLINE, AWAY states. For each enum, specify allowed values, default assignments, and business logic implications. Document how these enums are persisted as STRING values in the database and their impact on query performance. Include examples of enum usage in entity mappings, service logic, and API responses. Explain validation rules, transition constraints, and state machine behaviors. Address backward compatibility considerations for enum modifications and migration strategies for existing data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Delivery and Read Receipt Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the delivery and read receipt tracking system. Explain the implementation of MessageDeliveryUpdateDTO for tracking message delivery acknowledgments and MessageSeenUpdateDTO for read receipts. Document the UnreadCountDTO structure for managing unread message counts per user and chat room. Detail the integration with WebSocket broadcasting for real-time receipt updates to message participants. Explain the backend service methods that handle receipt updates, including database persistence and cache invalidation. Include concrete examples showing receipt flow from initial message send to final read confirmation. Document the relationship between receipt tracking and user presence systems. Address performance considerations for high-volume messaging scenarios and efficient receipt aggregation. Provide implementation guidelines for customizing receipt behavior and adding new receipt types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Chatify frontend system focused on the React 19 application structure and modern frontend patterns. Describe the component hierarchy with clear organization of chat components, common UI elements, sidebar functionality, and group management features. Document state management using React Context providers for authentication and WebSocket connections, custom hooks for reusable functionality, and routing configuration with React Router. Explain the build system using Vite, component composition patterns, and real-time communication implementation with WebSocket clients. Include infrastructure requirements for development environment, build optimization, and deployment considerations. Provide system context showing how the frontend handles user interface updates, real-time message synchronization, typing indicators, and presence status display. Address cross-cutting concerns like error handling, loading states, responsive design, and performance optimization through virtualized rendering. Document the technology stack including React ecosystem dependencies, styling with Tailwind CSS, and build tool configuration.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Chatify system architecture focused on describing the high-level design and component interactions. Document the layered architecture with presentation layer (React frontend), business logic layer (Spring Boot services), data access layer (JPA repositories), and infrastructure layer (external services). Explain the real-time communication architecture using WebSocket/STOMP protocol for instant messaging, with Kafka serving as the event bus for asynchronous processing. Detail the microservices-like separation within a single application, showing how authentication, messaging, file storage, and presence tracking are modularized. Document the data flow from user actions through WebSocket connections, Kafka message processing, database persistence, and real-time UI updates. Include system boundaries showing internal components versus external dependencies like AWS S3, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Kafka. Explain architectural patterns including Repository pattern, Observer pattern for real-time updates, Factory pattern for JWT utilities, and Event-driven architecture. Address scalability considerations, performance optimization strategies, and fault tolerance mechanisms. Provide component interaction diagrams and data flow illustrations to help developers understand the system's structure.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Chatify backend focused on JPA entities, repository patterns, and database relationships. Detail the entity relationships including Message, ChatRoom, User, UserChatState, and RefreshToken entities with their field definitions, data types, and constraints. Document the enum types including MessageStatus, MessageType, and UserStatus with their business meanings and usage contexts. Explain the repository layer implementation with custom query methods, pagination support, and transaction management. Include database schema diagrams showing table relationships, foreign key constraints, and indexing strategies. Document data access patterns, query optimization techniques, and caching strategies. Specify data lifecycle management, cascading operations, and entity state transitions. Address data validation rules, business constraints, and referential integrity enforcement. Include examples of complex queries, bulk operations, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Features Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Chatify real-time features implementation focused on WebSocket integration and live communication patterns. Document the useWebSocket.js hook implementation including WebSocket connection establishment, message subscription patterns, automatic reconnection with exponential backoff, and error handling strategies. Explain the useTypingIndicator.js hook for managing typing indicator state, debouncing user input, and broadcasting typing events to other participants. Detail the TypingIndicator.jsx component implementation showing real-time typing notifications with animated displays and participant management. Document the OnlineStatus.jsx component for displaying user presence status and handling online/offline state changes. Include the message synchronization patterns, real-time updates handling, and optimistic UI updates. Explain the WebSocket message formats, event types, and data transformation between server and client. Provide examples of real-time state management, conflict resolution for concurrent updates, and graceful degradation when WebSocket connections fail. Address performance optimization for real-time features, memory management for long-lived connections, and debugging techniques for WebSocket issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Message API endpoints focused on message sending, retrieval, delivery receipts, and read receipt functionality. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/messages, /api/messages/{id}, /api/messages/chat/{chatId}), request/response schemas including MessageDTO, SendMessageDTO, and delivery/read receipt structures. Explain message sending workflows, message retrieval with pagination, delivery acknowledgment mechanisms, and read receipt tracking. Include message status management (SENT, DELIVERED, SEEN), message threading, and chat history retrieval. Provide examples for sending different message types, handling delivery confirmations, and implementing read receipts. Address message ordering guarantees, duplicate detection, message archiving, and performance optimization for message retrieval queries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client-side WebSocket Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for client-side WebSocket integration in the React frontend application. Explain the WebSocketContext implementation for managing WebSocket connections, authentication state, and real-time message handling across the application. Document the useWebSocket custom hook for encapsulating WebSocket functionality, connection lifecycle management, and reconnection logic. Detail the websocket.js service module for low-level WebSocket operations, message serialization, and protocol handling. Include concrete examples showing WebSocket connection establishment, message sending and receiving patterns, and error handling strategies. Explain the integration with React state management, component lifecycle, and authentication context. Document reconnection mechanisms, connection state monitoring, and graceful degradation when WebSocket connectivity is lost. Address browser compatibility considerations, security implications of WebSocket connections, and performance optimization techniques for real-time messaging. Include troubleshooting guides for common WebSocket connection issues and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Access Patterns and Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the data access patterns and performance optimization strategies used in the Chatify application. Detail the JPA repository implementations for Message, ChatRoom, User, and UserChatState entities including custom query methods, pagination strategies, and indexing optimizations. Explain caching strategies using Redis for user presence, chat room metadata, and frequently accessed data. Document query optimization techniques including eager vs lazy loading, batch fetching, and N+1 query prevention. Cover transaction management, connection pooling, and database performance monitoring. Include examples of complex queries for message history retrieval, unread message counts, and real-time presence updates. Document data lifecycle management including message retention policies, user data cleanup, and archive procedures. Address scalability considerations for high-volume messaging scenarios and horizontal scaling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Reliability and Error Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for system reliability and error handling in the message delivery system. Explain the Kafka error handler configuration for managing failed message deliveries and retry mechanisms. Document the ApplicationStartupListener implementation for system initialization and health checks. Detail the GlobalExceptionHandler configuration for consistent error responses across the message delivery pipeline. Explain message deduplication strategies, idempotent processing, and eventual consistency patterns. Document retry policies, dead letter queue configuration, and circuit breaker patterns for fault tolerance. Include monitoring and alerting strategies for message delivery failures, consumer lag, and system health. Address graceful degradation scenarios and fallback mechanisms. Provide troubleshooting guides for common reliability issues including message ordering violations, consumer rebalancing, and system overload conditions. Document logging strategies and observability patterns for debugging complex delivery scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Chatify REST API endpoints focused on comprehensive coverage of all public interfaces. For authentication APIs, document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication methods including JWT token handling and OAuth2 Google integration. For user management APIs, document user search, status updates, presence information, and online user retrieval. For chat room APIs, document creation, member management, and chat room operations. For message APIs, document sending, receiving, delivery receipts, and read receipt functionality. For file APIs, document upload, download, and URL generation processes. Include protocol-specific examples with request/response samples, error handling strategies, security considerations, rate limiting, and authentication requirements. Document common use cases, client implementation guidelines, and performance optimization tips. Address API versioning, backwards compatibility, and debugging approaches. Provide curl examples and integration patterns for each endpoint group.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Processing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the message processing system focused on event-driven architecture, Kafka integration, and message state management. Thoroughly explain the Kafka producer service implementation for asynchronous message publishing to topics including message delivery and read receipt updates. Document the Kafka consumer service for consuming messages, processing business logic, and triggering WebSocket notifications. Detail the message service implementation including message validation, state transitions, and persistence operations. Explain the message DTOs and their role in data transfer between layers and external APIs. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing message flow from creation to delivery confirmation. Document the message state machine with SENT, DELIVERED, and SEEN states and their transitions. Address error handling, retry mechanisms, and dead letter queue processing. Explain the integration between Kafka message processing and WebSocket real-time updates for immediate user feedback.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration and Service Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Chatify API integration and service layer focused on HTTP client configuration and RESTful API communication patterns. Document the axios.js configuration including base URL setup, request/response interceptors, authentication token injection, and error handling middleware. Explain the API service modules for different resource types: auth.js for authentication endpoints, messages.js for message operations, chatrooms.js for chat room management, and users.js for user-related operations. Detail the service layer abstraction in api.js providing unified interface for API calls with consistent error handling and response formatting. Include authentication interceptor implementation for automatic JWT token attachment, request timeout configuration, and retry mechanisms for failed requests. Document the API response handling patterns, error mapping to user-friendly messages, and loading state management during API calls. Explain the data transformation between API responses and component state, request payload formatting, and query parameter handling. Provide examples of API service usage in components, error boundary implementation, and offline fallback strategies. Address security considerations for API communication, token refresh mechanisms, and rate limiting handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: File API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the File API endpoints focused on file upload, download, and URL generation for file sharing functionality. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns (/api/files/upload, /api/files/download/{fileName}), request/response schemas including FileUploadResponseDTO and multipart file handling. Explain file upload workflows with AWS S3 integration, file size limitations, supported file types, and security considerations. Include file URL generation, temporary access tokens, file deletion mechanisms, and storage management. Provide examples for uploading different file types, generating secure download links, and handling upload errors. Address file security validation, virus scanning integration, storage quotas, and CDN integration for file delivery optimization.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the WebSocket integration system focused on real-time communication implementation. Document the message handling architecture including inbound message processing, outbound message broadcasting, and subscription management. Detail the real-time features including message delivery receipts, typing indicators, read receipts, and online presence tracking. Explain relationships with other components including authentication integration, message processing pipeline, and presence tracking. Address common issues like connection drops, reconnection strategies, and message ordering guarantees. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers implementing real-time features.

wiki_catalogs.name: File Storage Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the file storage integration focused on AWS S3 configuration, file upload processing, and URL generation. Explain the FileStorageService implementation including S3 client configuration, bucket management, and file upload/download operations. Document the FileController REST endpoints for handling file uploads, URL generation, and file access. wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Chatify database schema focused on entity relationships and field definitions. Detail the core entities including Message, ChatRoom, User, and UserChatState with their relationships, field definitions, and data types. Document primary keys, foreign keys, indexes, and constraints that ensure data integrity and query performance. Specify data lifecycle including message retention policies, user data management, and cleanup procedures. Address data security requirements, access control patterns, and audit trail considerations. Include migration strategies for schema changes and version management approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: Presence and Caching
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the presence and caching system focused on Redis configuration, user presence tracking, and real-time status updates. Thoroughly explain the Redis configuration including connection pooling, serialization settings, and cache key management. Document the PresenceService implementation for tracking user online/offline status, typing indicators, and unread message counts. Detail the integration with WebSocket connections for real-time presence updates and the typing indicator system. Include concrete examples of presence data structures, cache expiration policies, and pub/sub messaging for status updates. Explain the relationship between presence tracking and message delivery receipts including unread count calculations and read status propagation. Document performance optimization techniques for high-concurrency scenarios and memory management strategies. Address consistency guarantees, eventual consistency patterns, and conflict resolution for distributed presence tracking across multiple instances.

wiki_catalogs.name: Message Delivery System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the message delivery system focused on the event-driven architecture and message state management. Explain the message state machine with SENT, DELIVERED, and SEEN states, including state transition logic and delivery acknowledgment mechanisms. Document the Kafka integration for asynchronous message processing, including producer configuration, consumer groups, and message serialization. Detail the delivery receipt system that tracks when messages are delivered and read, including DTO structures and update mechanisms. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing message flow from creation to final read confirmation. Document configuration options for Kafka topics, message retention, and processing guarantees. Explain relationships with WebSocket broadcasting, database persistence, and real-time notification systems. Address common issues like message ordering, duplicate processing, and failure recovery. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers implementing reliable messaging systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment documentation for the Chatify application focused on production-ready deployment strategies. Explain the Docker containerization approach with multi-container orchestration using docker-compose for backend, frontend, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Kafka services. Document environment variable configuration for production settings, database connection pooling, and external service integration. Detail the build process for both backend and frontend including Maven packaging, npm build optimization, and asset compilation. Include infrastructure requirements for cloud deployment, scaling considerations for WebSocket connections, and monitoring setup recommendations. Provide step-by-step deployment instructions for various environments including Docker Swarm, Kubernetes, and cloud platforms. Address security considerations for production deployment including SSL/TLS configuration, JWT security settings, and database hardening. Document backup strategies, disaster recovery procedures, and maintenance operations. Include troubleshooting guides for common deployment issues and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation for the Chatify application focused on quality assurance and testing best practices. Explain the backend testing strategy using JUnit and Spring Boot Test annotations, including unit testing for services, integration testing for controllers, and database testing with test containers. Document the frontend testing approach using React Testing Library, mock services for API testing, and component testing strategies. Detail the test configuration in pom.xml and package.json including test dependencies, coverage reporting, and continuous integration setup. Include concrete examples of testing WebSocket functionality, authentication flows, and real-time message handling. Document testing patterns for async operations, error handling scenarios, and edge cases. Address performance testing considerations, load testing strategies, and automated testing pipelines. Provide guidelines for writing effective tests, maintaining test suites, and integrating testing into the development workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation for the Chatify application focused on common issues and their solutions. Document WebSocket connection problems including CORS configuration, JWT token validation, and network connectivity issues. Address authentication failures covering token expiration, OAuth2 callback issues, and credential validation problems. Detail database connectivity issues including connection pooling, schema permissions, and migration failures. Include frontend troubleshooting for build errors, API connection problems, and real-time communication issues. Provide systematic debugging approaches for each component including backend logging, frontend console inspection, and network analysis. Document common error messages with their causes and resolution steps. Include performance troubleshooting for slow message delivery, memory leaks, and resource contention. Address deployment issues including container startup problems, environment configuration errors, and service dependency failures. Provide diagnostic tools, log analysis techniques, and escalation procedures for complex issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive contributing guidelines for the Chatify project focused on development workflow and code standards. Explain the development environment setup including IDE configuration, code formatting rules, and local development procedures. Document code quality standards including Java coding conventions, React component patterns, and ESLint configuration. Detail the pull request process including branch naming conventions, commit message standards, and review procedures. Include testing requirements for new features, documentation standards for API changes, and changelog maintenance procedures. Address issue reporting guidelines including bug report templates, feature request processes, and community contribution expectations. Document the release process, versioning strategy, and backward compatibility considerations. Provide guidelines for different types of contributions including bug fixes, feature enhancements, documentation improvements, and performance optimizations. Include contact information for project maintainers and community support channels.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Chatify: Real-Time Chat Application - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Chatify is a modern, real-time chat application built with a full-stack architecture featuring a Spring Boot backend and React frontend. The application provides instant messaging capabilities with advanced features including message delivery receipts, online presence tracking, file sharing, and group chat functionality.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Real-time Communication**: Enable instant messaging with WebSocket-based bidirectional communication
- **Reliable Message Delivery**: Implement robust message delivery and read receipts system
- **Scalable Architecture**: Utilize event-driven architecture with Kafka for message processing
- **Modern Authentication**: Support OAuth2 Google authentication with JWT token management
- **Performance Optimization**: Leverage Redis for caching and presence tracking

### Target Audience
- End-users seeking a modern chat experience
- Development teams looking for a reference implementation of real-time messaging systems
- Organizations requiring scalable chat infrastructure

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[React Frontend] --> B[Spring Boot Backend]
    B --> C[PostgreSQL Database]
    B --> D[Redis Cache]
    B --> E[Kafka Message Bus]
    E --> F[Kafka Consumers]
    F --> G[Message Processing]
    G --> H[WebSocket Broadcasting]
    B --> I[AWS S3 Storage]
    
    A --> J[SockJS Client]
    J --> K[STOMP Protocol]
    K --> L[WebSocket Endpoints]
```

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key architectural patterns:

**1. Event-Driven Architecture**
- Uses Kafka for decoupled message processing
- Enables horizontal scaling and fault tolerance
- Supports message replay and processing guarantees

**2. Repository Pattern**
- Clean separation between data access and business logic
- Consistent CRUD operations across entities
- Testable and maintainable code structure

**3. Observer Pattern**
- WebSocket connections for real-time updates
- Event broadcasting to connected clients
- Automatic UI updates without manual polling

**4. Factory Pattern**
- JWT token creation and validation
- Service instantiation based on configuration
- Flexible authentication mechanisms

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as React Client
    participant WS as WebSocket
    participant Kafka as Kafka Producer
    participant Consumer as Kafka Consumer
    participant DB as PostgreSQL
    
    Client->>+WS: Send Message
    WS->>+Kafka: Publish Event
    Kafka->>+Consumer: Process Message
    Consumer->>+DB: Save Message
    DB-->>-Consumer: Success
    Consumer-->>-WS: Broadcast to Users
    WS-->>-Client: Real-time Update
```

### Data Flow Diagrams

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Frontend Validation]
    B --> C[WebSocket Connection]
    C --> D[Message Controller]
    D --> E[Kafka Producer]
    E --> F[Kafka Topic]
    F --> G[Kafka Consumer]
    G --> H[Message Service]
    H --> I[Database Persistence]
    I --> J[WebSocket Broadcast]
    J --> K[Connected Clients]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application Entry Point**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/ChatBackendApplication.java` - Spring Boot main application class

**Frontend Application Entry Point**
- `chatify-frontend/src/App.jsx` - React application root component with routing configuration

### Core Modules

**WebSocket Configuration**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/config/WebSocketConfig.java` - STOMP endpoint configuration with JWT authentication

**Message Processing Pipeline**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/service/KafkaProducerService.java` - Asynchronous message publishing to Kafka topics
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/service/KafkaConsumerService.java` - Message consumption and processing

**Authentication System**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/security/JwtUtil.java` - JWT token generation and validation
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/security/JwtAuthenticationFilter.java` - HTTP request filtering for JWT validation

**Real-time Messaging**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/controller/ChatWebSocketController.java` - WebSocket endpoints for chat operations
- `chatify-frontend/src/context/WebSocketContext.jsx` - Frontend WebSocket client with automatic reconnection

### Configuration Approach

**Spring Boot Configuration**
- `src/main/resources/application.yml` - Environment-specific configuration management
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/config/` - Modular configuration classes for different subsystems

**Docker Configuration**
- `docker-compose.yml` - Multi-container orchestration for development and production
- `Dockerfile` - Containerization of the Spring Boot application

### External Dependencies

**Backend Dependencies**
- `pom.xml` - Maven dependency management including Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, and AWS SDK

**Frontend Dependencies**
- `chatify-frontend/package.json` - React ecosystem with Vite build tooling and Tailwind CSS styling

### Integration Points

**Database Integration**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/repository/` - JPA repositories for data persistence
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/entity/` - JPA entities with comprehensive mapping

**Cloud Storage Integration**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/service/FileStorageService.java` - AWS S3 integration for file uploads

**Authentication Integration**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/security/OAuth2SuccessHandler.java` - Google OAuth2 integration

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[AuthController] --> B[AuthService]
    C[MessageController] --> D[MessageService]
    E[ChatRoomController] --> F[ChatRoomService]
    G[FileController] --> H[FileStorageService]
    
    B --> I[UserRepository]
    D --> J[MessageRepository]
    F --> K[ChatRoomRepository]
    H --> L[AWS S3]
    
    M[WebSocketConfig] --> N[ChatWebSocketController]
    O[PresenceService] --> P[Redis]
    Q[KafkaProducerService] --> R[Kafka]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Real-time Messaging**
- Instant message delivery with WebSocket connections
- Message typing indicators and online presence
- File upload support with AWS S3 integration

**Advanced Message States**
- Message delivery receipts (DELIVERED)
- Message read receipts (SEEN)
- Offline message handling and synchronization

**User Management**
- OAuth2 Google authentication
- JWT token-based session management
- User presence tracking and status updates

**Group Chat Capabilities**
- Multi-user chat rooms
- Group member management
- Role-based permissions

### Implementation Highlights

**Message Delivery System**
- `MESSAGE_DELIVERY_DESIGN.md` - Comprehensive design documentation for message state management
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/entity/enums/MessageStatus.java` - Enumerated message states
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/dto/MessageDeliveryUpdateDTO.java` - Delivery acknowledgment DTO

**WebSocket Architecture**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/controller/ChatWebSocketController.java` - Centralized WebSocket message handling
- `chatify-frontend/src/context/WebSocketContext.jsx` - Robust frontend WebSocket client with automatic reconnection

**Performance Optimization**
- `src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/config/RedisConfig.java` - Redis integration for caching and presence tracking
- `chatify-frontend/src/components/Chat/VirtualizedMessageList.jsx` - Performance-optimized message rendering

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Authenticated
    Authenticated --> Connected: WebSocket Connect
    Connected --> Messaging: Room Join
    Messaging --> Delivery: Message Sent
    Delivery --> Seen: Message Read
    Seen --> Messaging: Continue Chatting
    Messaging --> Disconnected: WebSocket Disconnect
    Disconnected --> Connected: Reconnect
    Disconnected --> Authenticated: Token Refresh
    Authenticated --> [*]: Logout
```

**Message State Machine**
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> SENT
    SENT --> DELIVERED: Recipient ACK
    DELIVERED --> SEEN: Recipient Open Chat
    SEEN --> [*]: Complete
    
    note right of SENT: Message persisted in DB
    note right of DELIVERED: Recipient received
    note right of SEEN: Recipient viewed
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary

Chatify demonstrates a well-architected real-time messaging system that successfully combines modern technologies to deliver a scalable and reliable chat experience. The application showcases:

- **Event-Driven Design**: Kafka-based message processing enables horizontal scalability and fault tolerance
- **Real-time Communication**: WebSocket integration with STOMP protocol provides seamless bidirectional communication
- **Robust Authentication**: JWT-based token management with OAuth2 Google integration
- **Performance Optimization**: Redis caching and virtualized rendering for optimal user experience

### Architectural Strengths

1. **Modular Design**: Clear separation of concerns with dedicated modules for authentication, messaging, file storage, and presence tracking
2. **Scalability**: Event-driven architecture allows for easy horizontal scaling
3. **Maintainability**: Clean code structure with comprehensive documentation and testing
4. **Security**: Multi-layered authentication and authorization mechanisms
5. **Performance**: Optimized data structures and efficient rendering strategies

### Potential Improvement Areas

1. **Monitoring and Observability**: Consider adding distributed tracing and metrics collection
2. **Message Retention**: Implement configurable message retention policies
3. **Push Notifications**: Add mobile push notification support for offline users
4. **Message Encryption**: Implement end-to-end encryption for enhanced privacy
5. **Rate Limiting**: Add rate limiting mechanisms to prevent abuse

### Actionable Recommendations

1. **Implement Health Checks**: Add comprehensive health monitoring endpoints for all microservices
2. **Add Circuit Breaker Pattern**: Implement resilience patterns for external service dependencies
3. **Enhance Testing**: Expand unit and integration test coverage, particularly for WebSocket functionality
4. **Performance Profiling**: Conduct load testing to identify bottlenecks under high concurrency
5. **Documentation Enhancement**: Create API documentation with OpenAPI/Swagger integration

### Next Steps for Project Evolution

1. **Microservices Migration**: Consider breaking down monolithic architecture into smaller, focused services
2. **Mobile Application**: Develop native mobile clients for iOS and Android platforms
3. **WebRTC Integration**: Add voice and video calling capabilities
4. **Cross-platform Deployment**: Enhance Docker configurations for cloud-native deployments
5. **Advanced Analytics**: Implement usage analytics and user behavior tracking

The Chatify project serves as an excellent foundation for building enterprise-grade real-time communication systems, demonstrating best practices in modern full-stack development while maintaining scalability and maintainability.

---

**Sources:**
- [ChatBackendApplication.java](src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/ChatBackendApplication.java)
- [pom.xml](pom.xml)
- [package.json](chatify-frontend/package.json)
- [App.jsx](chatify-frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [WebSocketConfig.java](src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/config/WebSocketConfig.java)
- [ChatWebSocketController.java](src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/controller/ChatWebSocketController.java)
- [WebSocketContext.jsx](chatify-frontend/src/context/WebSocketContext.jsx)
- [docker-compose.yml](docker-compose.yml)
- [MESSAGE_DELIVERY_DESIGN.md](MESSAGE_DELIVERY_DESIGN.md)
- [KafkaProducerService.java](src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/service/KafkaProducerService.java)
- [Message.java](src/main/java/com/chatify/chat_backend/entity/Message.java)
- [vite.config.js](chatify-frontend/vite.config.js)

</blog>
